summary: Heavy week continues: BeyondTrust CVE-2026-1731 confirmed in ransomware campaigns with full technical analysis published. Microsoft Patch Tuesday includes 6 actively exploited zero-days. AI-assisted attacks at scale (600+ FortiGate devices). First Android malware using GenAI at runtime (PromptSpy). Starkiller PhaaS enables real-time MFA bypass. AISLE AI found all 12 OpenSSL zero-days.

critical.title: BeyondTrust RS/PRA Command Injection — Ransomware Confirmed
critical.affected: BeyondTrust Remote Support, Privileged Remote Access
critical.action: Patch immediately, assume compromise if unpatched past 2026-02-16

critical.title: Microsoft Patch Tuesday — 6 Zero-Days
critical.affected: Windows (all supported versions), Word, RDS, DWM, RRAS

critical.title: Dell RecoverPoint for VMs — Hard-Coded Credentials
critical.action: Patch or isolate — CISA deadline overdue

high.title: AI-Assisted FortiGate Campaign — 600+ Devices in 55 Countries
high.action: Audit FortiGate management exposure, enforce MFA

high.title: Ivanti Zero-Day Exploitation Surge
high.action: Review Ivanti patching, hunt for IOCs

notable.title: PromptSpy — First Android Malware Using GenAI at Runtime
notable.significance: Novel threat class — LLM-assisted runtime adaptation

notable.title: Starkiller PhaaS — Real-Time MFA Bypass via Reverse Proxy
notable.significance: Commoditizes MFA bypass for unsophisticated attackers

notable.title: Kimwolf Botnet Sybil Attack on I2P Network
SLSH Extortion Group Escalation — Swatting, Harassment

trends: AI in offense accelerating: AI-assisted campaigns at scale, AI-generated malware, AI runtime malware adaptation
AI in defense: AISLE found all 12 OpenSSL zero-days — unprecedented concentration
Supply chain attacks continue (Cline CLI npm compromise)
Ransomware driving urgent CISA KEV timelines
